svg.title: LLM flaws and their fixes — overview map
svg.desc: Six core LLM flaws arranged in two columns: hallucination, context window limit, no real-time knowledge, bias, no true reasoning, and high compute cost. Each paired with its primary fix category.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do LLMs hallucinate and what causes it?')
sendPrompt('How does RAG fix LLM hallucination?')
sendPrompt('What is the LLM context window limitation?')
sendPrompt('How do long-context models and memory solve the context window problem?')
sendPrompt('Why do LLMs have a knowledge cutoff date?')
sendPrompt('How does web search tool use fix LLM knowledge cutoff?')
sendPrompt('What kinds of bias exist in LLMs and where do they come from?')
sendPrompt('How does RLHF and data curation reduce LLM bias?')
sendPrompt('Why do LLMs fail at multi-step logical reasoning?')
sendPrompt('How does chain-of-thought prompting improve LLM reasoning?')
sendPrompt('Why are LLMs so expensive to run and train?')
sendPrompt('How do quantization and distillation make LLMs cheaper?')

svg.text: Click any box to go deeper
